markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's repeated delays of Grand Theft Auto VI, now officially slated for November 19, 2026 after shifts from fall 2025 and a brief May target, have extended the window for preceding events and heightened trader focus on cultural milestones. Recent pre-order leaks and earnings-call signals point to an imminent trailer 3 drop, while industry emphasis on polish and console exclusivity keeps the console launch as the resolution benchmark. Momentum favors high-probability outcomes like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire and Drake's Iceman album release, driven by ongoing geopolitical talks and the rapper's recent singles, with traders viewing the extended timeline as a near-certainty for these developments to precede launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has sharply reinforced trader consensus around no additional prison time, as the deadlocked jury leaves the remaining charges in limbo amid his already lengthy incarceration. At 73 and in declining health, including documented heart issues and mobility limitations requiring a wheelchair, the former producer’s legal team is positioned to argue for release or minimal further penalties through ongoing California appeals and time-served calculations. Historical patterns in high-profile #MeToo cases show courts factoring age and medical frailty into sentencing, which traders view as tilting heavily against extended terms. An upset could emerge only if prosecutors secure a swift retrial victory or California appeals fail outright, but both paths face steep procedural and evidentiary hurdles.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely bunched trader consensus around the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes reflects their dominant 2025-26 regular seasons and strong playoff positioning. The Avalanche posted the NHL’s best record at 55-16-11 while leading the Western Conference, and they opened the postseason with a sweep of the Kings before advancing in the second round. Carolina finished second overall with a 53-22-7 mark, claimed the top Eastern seed, and has yet to drop a game through eight playoff contests. These factors, combined with deep rosters and favorable matchups ahead, keep implied probabilities for both clubs separated by just over two points. Vegas, Montreal, and Buffalo trail as longer shots, their lower shares shaped by tougher remaining paths and less consistent recent form.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, England, Brazil, and Argentina reflects a wide-open field driven by recent international results and squad depth ahead of the expanded 48-team tournament. Spain’s status as European champions and unbeaten run in major competitions has kept them neck-and-neck with France, whose attacking talent pool remains elite despite isolated injury absences. If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Democratic presidential nomination for 2028 features a fragmented field with no dominant frontrunner, as traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.1 percent while spreading the balance across dozens of alternatives. Leading contenders stand out through differing combinations of statewide executive records, national name recognition, and recent electoral performance in battleground states. Newsom’s position reflects his visibility as California governor and fundraising capacity, whereas Kamala Harris and Pete Buttigieg draw residual support from prior national campaigns. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Jon Ossoff benefit from strong progressive and Southern bases, respectively. Consolidation around one outcome would likely require sustained polling gains in early primary states, major endorsements from party figures, or demonstrated strength among key voting blocs ahead of the 2028 convention.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Democratic presidential nomination for 2028 features a fragmented field with no dominant frontrunner, as traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.1 percent while spreading the balance across dozens of alternatives. Leading contenders stand out through differing combinations of statewide executive records, national name recognition, and recent electoral performance in battleground states. Newsom’s position reflects his visibility as California governor and fundraising capacity, whereas Kamala Harris and Pete Buttigieg draw residual support from prior national campaigns. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Jon Ossoff benefit from strong progressive and Southern bases, respectively. Consolidation around one outcome would likely require sustained polling gains in early primary states, major endorsements from party figures, or demonstrated strength among key voting blocs ahead of the 2028 convention.
